title: Mombasa: Key outcomes from the Our Ocean Conference in Kenya
content: A major ocean conference has ended in Mombasa, Kenya, with just a handful of countries committing to high-level political declarations on banning deep-sea mining, protecting climate-resilient coral reefs and combatting illegal fishing.

creator: Giuliana Viglione
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Most of the world’s wealth belongs to all of us. It’s time to start collecting
content: As extreme inequality worsens and AI concentrates wealth into the hands of a small elite, insisting that the wealth generated by our collective inheritance be shared with all its rightful beneficiaries is not so much a political position as a moral imperative.

creator: Jeremy Lent

title: In defense of “nature”: why the word still matters in the age of AI
content: "Nature" is often cast as a word that separates humans from non-humans. But it can also mean something much larger: the universe, the course of things, the great event we live within. It's a word worth defending.

creator: Rob Lewis

title: For the Love of Our Children: a poem by Esther Phillips
content: Environmental activist and poet Esther Phillips reflects on grief and anti-natalism in this poem, which explores the choice not to have children as an act of love in an age of collapse.
snippet: Environmental activist and poet Esther Phillips reflects on grief and anti-natalism in this poem, which explores the choice not to have children as an act of love in an age of collapse.

title: Inside an off-grid village of earthen domes – YouTube documentary review
content: Built from stacked earthbags and low-cost materials drawn largely from the surrounding landscape, the Mojave Center showcases an unconventional approach to homebuilding, constructed with the help of just a few volunteers. This documentary explores a compelling vision of off-grid life in the Arizona desert.

creator: Frank Kaminski

title: Climate diplomacy glossary: Learn the lingo of international climate talks with this handy reference guide
content: UN climate diplomacy can be hard to follow. This glossary explains the key terms, groups and forums shaping the talks.
snippet: UN climate diplomacy can be hard to follow. This glossary explains the key terms, groups and forums shaping the talks.
creator: Karl Mathiesen

title: From Mississippi to Yellowstone: What headwaters teach about designing bioregional funding ecosystems
content: Imagine what a financial system would do if it behaved like a headwaters system. There would be continual arrival of flows from elsewhere. Resources would pool into local places. Actions would converge in the same direction. And all of it would support the continual emergence and maintenance of life.

creator: Joe Brewer

title: UN Climate Change Conference 2026: Latest global climate talks end with little progress amid deep political tensions
content: Two weeks of tense UN climate talks in Bonn, Germany, have produced few tangible outcomes as diplomats faced “gridlock”.
snippet: Two weeks of tense UN climate talks in Bonn, Germany, have produced few tangible outcomes as diplomats faced “gridlock”.
creator: Josh Gabbatiss

title: The most appropriate response to falling birthrates? Embrace them
content: As governments push costly pronatalist schemes to boost birthrates, the real solution, argues Nandita Bajaj, is to embrace declining fertility, expand women’s reproductive agency, and redesign economies for equity and ecological survival.
snippet: As governments push costly pronatalist schemes to boost birthrates, the real solution, argues Nandita Bajaj, is to embrace declining fertility, expand women’s reproductive agency, and redesign

title: The consumer power myth: Why it’s time to de-commodify food
content: The impression that we can eat what we want fits neatly with a neoliberal story that treats capitalism as democratic, where people “vote with their wallets”. But this is an illusion. Rather than putting our faith in green consumerism to rebuild food systems, we should strive to de‑commodify food.

creator: Gunnar Rundgren

title: Escalating climate and geopolitical shocks force a choice: build resilience or be shaped by crisis
content: Britain is in the grip of its worst-ever, record-shattering midsummer heatwave — even before the impacts of a predicted super El Niño of “Godzilla” strength are fully felt. In this stark context, Rupert Read examines what resilience-building must mean in an era of escalating climate extremes.

title: Women face the greatest climate risks but are critical to climate action
content: Women and girls are disproportionately affected by the climate crisis, yet women’s leadership and local knowledge are critical to building more resilient communities.
snippet: Women and girls are disproportionately affected by the climate crisis, yet women’s leadership and local knowledge are critical to building more resilient communities.
creator: Ruhi Bhasin
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Finding hope and purpose in a world in crisis
content: After years of turning her backyard into a haven for wildlife, the author, Cylvia Hayes, watched the songbirds suddenly disappear. What began as a personal loss became a reflection on eco-grief, ecological decline, and how to find meaning, connection, and hope in a world undergoing profound environmental change.

title: World Localization Day: Helena Norberg-Hodge on rebuilding local economies
content: In honor of World Localization Day, Helena Norberg-Hodge, founder of Local Futures and pioneer of the Localization movement, reflects on decades of work as an activist and the dangers that global, deregulated corporations pose to the global economy and the environmental movement.

title: How to end capitalism – and build a kind, green, cooperative economy. Cont’d
content: From climate policy to housing and trade, in this piece, author Guy Dauncey imagines how to dismantle capitalism and replace it with a greener, more cooperative alternative for everyone, including nature.

title: A lawful anarchist: The case against law in an age of mass extinction
content: We’re taught that without rigid legal systems society would collapse into chaos. But for most of human history, people relied on communal norms, not codified law, while the rise of written rules, hierarchy, and possessions cleared the way for today’s sixth mass extinction.

creator: Tom Murphy
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Feeding the World as if People Mattered: Why we need more farms, not more food – book excerpt
content: Asking if the world grows enough food is the wrong kind of question. It leads to the wrong kind of answer. We don’t need to produce more food. We need to produce more farms: places where communities of living beings can thrive.
